source: Goya Foods - goya.com | Allrecipes (15-min dinners, stir-fries, casseroles, one-pan dinners) | Cookpad Puerto Rico | Various
note: Recipes filtered for Kiki's IBS and personal preferences. Aceitunas/olives removed from fricase de pollo since Kiki hates them. No mayonnaise, no alfredo, no mac and cheese. Ham (jamón) and salchicha are allowed. Bacon is allowed. No lechón, pernil, chorizo, longaniza, or pork chops. Additional recipes from Allrecipes collections, Cookpad Puerto Rico, and other sources. All filtered for Kiki: no spicy foods, no pork (except bacon), no cheese on rice, no alfredo, no mayo, no olives. IBS-friendly. Bilingual English/Spanish.

recipes.name: Fricase de Pollo (Chicken Fricassee)
recipes.spanish_name: Fricase de Pollo
recipes.ibs_notes: Gentle stew, easy on the stomach. Skip the olives (aceitunas) since Kiki hates them. The ham (jamón de cocinar) in this recipe is fine — ham and salchicha are allowed. Potatoes and chicken are safe foods for Kiki.
ingredients: 1 whole chicken (3 lbs), cut into pieces
steps: Heat oil in a pot over medium heat. Add ham and brown. Add onion, pepper, garlic, oregano, and cilantro. Reduce heat to medium-low and cook 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.

Add chicken and cook over medium heat for 5 minutes. Stir in tomato sauce, bay leaf, vinegar, Adobo, and potatoes. Add 2.5 cups water. Bring to a boil, cover, reduce to low, cook 45 minutes until chicken is tender.
Stir in peas and optional butter. Cook uncovered 5 more minutes until sauce thickens.
recipes.serve_with: Arroz blanco (white rice)

recipes.name: Puerto Rican Rice and Beans (Arroz con Habichuelas)
recipes.spanish_name: Arroz con Habichuelas Rosadas
recipes.ibs_notes: Classic Puerto Rican staple. Very gentle on the stomach. One of Kiki's everyday foods.
steps: Heat sofrito in a saucepan over medium heat for 2 minutes.
Add pink beans with their liquid, tomato sauce, and Adobo. Stir well.
Bring to a boil, reduce heat, simmer 10 minutes until sauce thickens.
Serve over white rice.
recipes.serve_with: Arroz blanco, pechuga empanada, or any protein

recipes.name: Picadillo (Spiced Ground Beef)
recipes.spanish_name: Picadillo
recipes.ibs_notes: Kiki's classic carne molida. Skip the olives. Great over white rice or in tacos and burritos.
steps: Cook ground beef in a skillet over medium-high heat until browned. Drain excess fat.
Add sofrito and onion. Cook 3 minutes until softened.
Add tomato sauce, Adobo, and Sazon. Stir well.
Simmer over low heat 15 minutes until sauce thickens and flavors blend.
recipes.serve_with: Arroz blanco, tacos, burritos, quesadillas, or tostones

recipes.name: Arroz con Pollo (Chicken and Rice)
recipes.spanish_name: Arroz con Pollo
recipes.ibs_notes: Classic one-pot meal. Skip the alcaparrado/olives. Chicken and rice are both safe foods for Kiki.
ingredients: 1 whole chicken (3 lbs), cut into pieces
steps: Season chicken with Adobo. Heat oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Brown chicken on all sides, about 8 minutes. Remove and set aside.
In the same pot, cook sofrito, onion, pepper, and garlic over medium heat for 3 minutes.
Add rice and stir to coat. Add broth, tomato sauce, and Sazon. Return chicken to pot.
Bring to a boil, cover, reduce to low, cook 20 minutes until rice is tender and chicken is cooked through.
Stir in peas, cover, cook 5 more minutes.
recipes.serve_with: Tostones or a simple salad

recipes.name: Carne Guisada (Beef and Potato Stew)
recipes.spanish_name: Carne Guisada
recipes.ibs_notes: Hearty beef and potato stew. Slow cooked so the beef is very tender and easy to digest. Skip the alcaparrado. Potatoes and beef are Kiki favorites.
steps: Season beef with Adobo. Heat oil in a heavy pot over medium-high heat. Brown beef on all sides, about 5 minutes. Remove and set aside.
In the same pot, cook sofrito, onion, and garlic for 3 minutes.
Return beef to pot. Add tomato sauce, Sazon, water, and bay leaf. Bring to a boil.
Cover and simmer on low heat for 2 hours until beef is very tender.
Add potatoes and cook 30 more minutes until potatoes are soft and stew thickens.
recipes.serve_with: Arroz blanco and habichuelas

recipes.name: Habichuelas Guisadas (Stewed Red Beans)
recipes.spanish_name: Habichuelas Guisadas
recipes.ibs_notes: Puerto Rican classic. Very gentle and comforting. Essential side dish for Kiki.
steps: Heat olive oil in a saucepan. Add recaito, pepper, and onion. Cook 3 minutes.
Add beans with their liquid, tomato sauce, water, and Adobo.
Bring to a boil, reduce heat, simmer 20 minutes until sauce thickens.
Adjust seasoning with salt.
recipes.serve_with: Arroz blanco and any protein — especially pechuga empanada

recipes.name: Ropa Vieja (Cuban Shredded Beef Stew)
recipes.spanish_name: Ropa Vieja
recipes.ibs_notes: Tender shredded beef in a rich tomato sauce. Very easy to digest since the beef is slow-cooked until very soft. Skip any olives in the recipe.
ingredients: 2 lbs flank steak or beef brisket
steps: Season beef with Adobo. Heat oil in a large pot over high heat. Sear beef on all sides until browned.
Add onion, peppers, and garlic. Cook 5 minutes.
Add tomato sauce, sofrito, Sazon, bay leaf, and broth. Bring to a boil.
Cover and simmer on very low heat for 2.5 hours until beef is very tender and falls apart.
Remove beef and shred with two forks. Return to pot and mix with the sauce.
recipes.serve_with: Arroz blanco and tostones

recipes.name: Mamposteao (Puerto Rican Rice Mixed with Beans)
recipes.spanish_name: Mamposteao
recipes.ibs_notes: Puerto Rican comfort food — leftover rice mixed with beans. Simple, gentle, and delicious.
ingredients: 2 cups cooked white rice (leftover works great)
1 can (15.5 oz) pink beans with liquid
steps: Heat sofrito in a large pan over medium heat for 2 minutes.
Add beans with their liquid and tomato sauce. Season with Adobo. Simmer 5 minutes.
Add cooked rice and mix everything together.
Cook over medium heat 5-8 minutes, stirring, until the rice absorbs some of the bean sauce.
recipes.serve_with: Any protein — especially chicken or steak

recipes.name: Breaded Chicken (Pechuga Empanada)
recipes.spanish_name: Pechuga Empanada
recipes.ibs_notes: One of Kiki's absolute favorites. Fried breaded chicken — crispy outside, juicy inside. Use plain breadcrumbs and season well with Adobo.
steps: Season chicken breasts on both sides with Adobo, garlic powder, and onion powder.
Dip each breast in beaten egg, then coat in breadcrumbs. Press to adhere.
Heat about 1 inch of oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Fry chicken 4-5 minutes per side until golden brown and cooked through.
Drain on paper towels.
recipes.serve_with: Arroz blanco con habichuelas — Kiki's favorite combo

recipes.name: Lemon Garlic Chicken
recipes.spanish_name: Pollo al Limon con Ajo
recipes.ibs_notes: Light and bright — lemon is gentle on IBS. Baked version is easiest on the stomach. One of Kiki's favorites.
ingredients: Fresh parsley or cilantro to serve
steps: Mix lemon juice, garlic, olive oil, Adobo, and oregano. Marinate chicken in this mix for at least 30 minutes.
Preheat oven to 400°F. Place chicken in a baking dish with the marinade.
Bake 35-40 minutes until chicken is golden and cooked through.
Baste with pan juices halfway through cooking.
recipes.serve_with: Mashed potatoes or arroz blanco

recipes.name: Teriyaki Chicken
recipes.spanish_name: Pollo Teriyaki
recipes.ibs_notes: Sweet and savory. Use low-sodium soy sauce for IBS. One of Kiki's favorites. Great over rice.
ingredients: 1 tbsp cornstarch mixed with 2 tbsp water
steps: Mix soy sauce, honey, vinegar, garlic, and ginger in a bowl.
Heat oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Cook chicken 5-6 minutes per side until golden.
Pour teriyaki sauce over chicken. Simmer 3 minutes.
Add cornstarch mixture and stir until sauce thickens and coats the chicken.
recipes.serve_with: White rice — classic Kiki combo

recipes.name: Mashed Potatoes
recipes.spanish_name: Pure de Papas
recipes.ibs_notes: One of the best IBS comfort foods. Potatoes are very gentle on the stomach. Butter and a little milk are usually well tolerated.
ingredients: 1/2 cup warm milk or chicken broth
steps: Boil potatoes in salted water 15-20 minutes until fork tender.
Drain well. Return to pot over low heat 1 minute to dry out.
Mash potatoes with butter until smooth.
Add warm milk or broth gradually, mashing until creamy. Season with garlic powder and white pepper.
recipes.serve_with: Lemon chicken, steak, or any protein

recipes.name: Locrio de Pollo (Dominican Chicken and Rice)
recipes.spanish_name: Locrio de Pollo
recipes.ibs_notes: Dominican version of arroz con pollo. Skip the olives. Chicken and rice together — very satisfying and easy to digest.
steps: Season chicken with Adobo. Heat oil and sugar in a large pot over medium-high heat until sugar caramelizes dark brown.
Add chicken and sear in the caramel until browned on all sides.
Add sofrito and cook 2 minutes. Add rice and stir to coat.
Add broth and Sazon. Bring to a boil, cover, reduce to low, cook 25 minutes.
Fluff with a fork before serving.
recipes.serve_with: Tostones and avocado slices

recipes.name: Teriyaki Chicken Noodle Bowl
recipes.spanish_name: Tazón de Pollo Teriyaki con Fideos
recipes.ibs_notes: Chicken thighs stay juicy and IBS-friendly. Use low-sodium soy sauce. Cook chicken, noodles, and sauce separately so nothing gets watery. Teriyaki sauce is gentle — no spice, no heat.
ingredients: 1 lb boneless skinless chicken thighs, cut into bite-size pieces
8 oz spaghetti, linguine, or udon noodles
Salt, pepper, garlic powder, onion powder
Sesame seeds and green onions to garnish
steps: Cook noodles per package directions. Drain and set aside.
Mix soy sauce, honey, brown sugar, vinegar, garlic, and ginger in a small bowl. Set aside.
Season chicken with salt, pepper, garlic powder, and onion powder. Cook in a hot oiled skillet over medium-high heat until golden and cooked through, about 5-6 minutes. Remove to a plate.
Pour teriyaki sauce into the same skillet. Bring to a simmer. Add cornstarch slurry and stir until sauce thickens and turns glossy, about 2 minutes.
Return chicken and noodles to the skillet. Toss everything together until coated. Serve garnished with sesame seeds and green onions.
recipes.serve_with: Arroz blanco on the side or just as a noodle bowl on its own
recipes.kiki_filter: APPROVED — no spice, no pork, no alfredo, chicken thighs are IBS-friendly. Skip any recipe version that adds sriracha or red pepper flakes.

recipes.name: Easy Ramen with Chicken and Teriyaki Sauce
recipes.spanish_name: Ramen Fácil con Pollo y Salsa Teriyaki
recipes.ibs_notes: Quick and light. Use shredded rotisserie chicken for even faster prep. Skip the ramen seasoning packets — they're very high sodium. Just use your own teriyaki sauce. Zucchini, carrots, and onion are IBS-friendly veggies here.
ingredients: 2 packs instant ramen noodles (seasoning packets discarded)
1.5 cups shredded cooked chicken breast or rotisserie chicken
steps: Cook ramen noodles in boiling water for 3 minutes. Drain and set aside.
Melt butter in a skillet over medium heat. Add carrot, zucchini, and onion. Sauté until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add shredded chicken and teriyaki sauce. Cook until heated through and sauce thickens slightly, about 3-5 minutes.
Add drained noodles to the skillet. Toss everything together. Add more teriyaki sauce as needed. Serve immediately.
recipes.serve_with: Great on its own. Add a side of arroz blanco if you want something heartier.
recipes.kiki_filter: APPROVED — quick, mild, no spice. Throw away the ramen flavor packets. Rotisserie chicken makes this even easier.

recipes.name: Asian Ground Beef Noodle Bowl (Carne Molida con Fideos)
recipes.spanish_name: Tazón de Carne Molida Estilo Asiático
recipes.ibs_notes: Ground beef (carne molida) is one of Kiki's favorites. This is basically a fusion of her comfort food with Asian flavors. Use ramen, spaghetti, or egg noodles. Skip the sriracha and red pepper flakes — season with soy sauce, garlic, and brown sugar only.
ingredients: Green onions and sesame seeds to garnish
steps: Cook noodles per package directions. Drain and set aside.
Mix soy sauce, brown sugar, garlic, and sesame oil in a small bowl. Set aside.
Brown ground beef in a large skillet over medium-high heat, breaking it up. Drain excess fat.
Add onion and cook 3 minutes until softened.
Pour sauce over the beef. Stir and simmer on low 5 minutes.
Toss in noodles and mix until everything is coated and heated through. Serve garnished with green onions and sesame seeds.
recipes.serve_with: Eat as a noodle bowl. Also great over arroz blanco instead of noodles — same sauce.
recipes.kiki_filter: APPROVED — carne molida is Kiki's protein, sauce is mild. Do NOT add sriracha, hoisin, fish sauce, or any spicy additions.

recipes.name: Garlic Mushroom Chicken Thigh Stir-Fry
recipes.spanish_name: Muslos de Pollo con Ajo y Champiñones
recipes.ibs_notes: Mushrooms and chicken thighs are IBS-friendly when cooked in butter and broth instead of cream. Skip the cream if dairy is a trigger — chicken broth alone makes a great sauce. The garlic-butter pan sauce is gentle and delicious.
steps: Season chicken thighs with salt, pepper, and thyme. Sear in a hot skillet with 1 tbsp butter over medium-high heat, about 4-5 minutes per side until golden. Remove to a plate.
In the same skillet, add remaining butter and mushrooms. Cook until golden, 4-6 minutes. Add garlic and cook 1 more minute.
Add flour and stir for 1 minute to cook it off. Pour in chicken broth, scraping up the browned bits from the pan. Stir well.
Return chicken to the pan. Cover and cook on low heat for 15-20 minutes until chicken is fully cooked through.
Spoon sauce and mushrooms over chicken. Garnish with parsley and serve.
recipes.serve_with: Mashed potatoes or arroz blanco — the garlic broth is perfect for soaking into both
recipes.kiki_filter: APPROVED — no spice, no pork, uses butter and broth not cream. Do NOT add red pepper flakes.

recipes.name: Garlic Shrimp Stir-Fry with White Rice
recipes.spanish_name: Camarones al Ajo con Arroz Blanco
recipes.ibs_notes: Shrimp is one of Kiki's approved proteins. Quick stir-fry with garlic and soy sauce — very gentle on the stomach. Serve over white rice for a classic Kiki combo. No spicy sauce needed — garlic + soy + butter is all the flavor it needs.
ingredients: 1 lb large shrimp, peeled and deveined
Green onions and lemon wedge to serve
steps: Season shrimp with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add shrimp in a single layer. Cook 1-2 minutes per side until pink and curled. Remove to a plate.
Add butter and garlic to the same pan. Sauté 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Return shrimp to the pan. Add soy sauce and toss to coat. Cook 30 seconds more.
Serve immediately over white rice. Squeeze lemon over the top.
recipes.serve_with: Arroz blanco — this is the move
recipes.kiki_filter: APPROVED — shrimp is on Kiki's safe list, garlic-butter sauce is IBS-friendly. Do NOT add hot sauce or chili.

recipes.name: Sheet Pan Steak and Potatoes
recipes.spanish_name: Bistec y Papas en Bandeja al Horno
recipes.ibs_notes: Steak and potatoes — two of Kiki's all-time favorites in one pan. Simple marinade of garlic, olive oil, and soy sauce. Baking instead of frying keeps it IBS-gentle. One pan = easy cleanup.
steps: Preheat oven to 425°F. Line a large baking sheet with foil.
Toss potato wedges with 2 tbsp olive oil, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Spread on one side of the baking sheet. Roast 20 minutes.
Mix remaining olive oil, garlic, and soy sauce. Brush over the steaks and season with salt and pepper.
Remove potatoes from the oven. Make space in the center of the pan and add the steaks. Return to oven and broil 4-5 minutes per side until done to your liking.
Let steaks rest 5 minutes before slicing. Serve with the roasted potatoes and garnish with parsley.
recipes.serve_with: Add mashed potatoes or a simple white rice on the side for even more comfort
recipes.kiki_filter: APPROVED — steak and potatoes are two of Kiki's comfort foods. Mild marinade, no spice. Easy and delicious.

recipes.name: 5-Ingredient Sheet Pan Chicken and Potatoes
recipes.spanish_name: Pollo y Papas en Bandeja (Solo 5 Ingredientes)
recipes.ibs_notes: As simple as it gets — chicken breast and potatoes in one pan. Season with adobo and garlic powder instead of ranch. IBS-friendly, low-fat, requires almost no prep. Great for tired days.
ingredients: 4 chicken breasts, pounded to even thickness
steps: Preheat oven to 425°F. Grease a large sheet pan.
Toss potatoes with olive oil, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Spread on the pan. Roast 15 minutes.
Season chicken breasts with adobo, salt, and pepper. Add to the pan with the potatoes.
Bake 20-25 minutes until chicken reaches 165°F internal temp. Switch to broil for 3-5 minutes for crispier tops.
Rest 5 minutes and serve.
recipes.serve_with: Perfect as is. Add arroz blanco and habichuelas on the side for a full Puerto Rican plate.
recipes.kiki_filter: APPROVED — minimal ingredients, adobo seasoning makes this feel boricua, no spice, no pork.

recipes.name: Ground Beef Baked Lasagna (Kiki's Favorite)
recipes.spanish_name: Lasaña de Carne Molida al Horno
recipes.source_url: allrecipes.com 9x13 ground beef casseroles + classic recipe
recipes.ibs_notes: Classic lasagna — one of Kiki's absolute favorites. No alfredo, no béchamel, just red sauce and meat. Use mozzarella and pizza blend on top only. Cheese goes between the layers and on top — not mixed into the rice/sides. Serve with arroz blanco on the side.
steps: Preheat oven to 375°F.
Brown ground beef in a skillet. Add garlic, tomato sauce, diced tomatoes, Italian seasoning, salt, and pepper. Simmer 10 minutes.
Mix ricotta with the egg, salt, and pepper in a bowl.

In a 9x13 dish, spread 1 cup of meat sauce on the bottom. Layer: noodles, ricotta mixture, meat sauce, mozzarella. Repeat layers. Finish with noodles, remaining sauce, and top with mozzarella and pizza blend cheese.
Cover with foil and bake 45 minutes. Remove foil and bake 15 more minutes until bubbly and golden.
Let rest 15 minutes before cutting.
recipes.serve_with: Arroz blanco on the side — Kiki's favorite combo
recipes.kiki_filter: APPROVED — no alfredo, cheese is only between layers and on top (not on rice). Ground beef, tomato sauce, mozzarella — all Kiki-safe ingredients.

recipes.name: Baked Chicken Thighs with Garlic and Herbs
recipes.spanish_name: Muslos de Pollo al Horno con Ajo y Hierbas
recipes.ibs_notes: Baked chicken thighs are one of the best IBS-friendly proteins — tender, juicy, and hard to mess up. Season with adobo and garlic. No spice needed. Great for meal prep.
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F.
Pat chicken thighs dry. Rub all over with olive oil, adobo, garlic powder, oregano, salt, and pepper.
Place skin-side up in a baking dish or on a sheet pan.
Bake 35-40 minutes until the skin is golden-crispy and the internal temperature reaches 165°F.
Rest 5 minutes before serving.
recipes.serve_with: Mashed potatoes, arroz blanco, or tostones — pairs with everything
recipes.kiki_filter: APPROVED — simple, flavorful, no spice, no pork. Great base recipe Kiki can dress up any way she wants.

recipes.name: One-Pan Chicken and White Rice Casserole
recipes.spanish_name: Casserole de Pollo y Arroz en Una Sola Bandeja
recipes.ibs_notes: One pan, minimal cleanup, very gentle on the stomach. The rice cooks right in the chicken broth inside the oven — no cream of mushroom soup needed. Cheddar on top is optional, not mixed into the rice.
ingredients: 1/2 cup shredded cheddar cheese (for the top — not mixed in)
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease a 9x13 pan.
Mix uncooked rice with chicken broth, garlic powder, onion powder, and salt in the pan.
Rub chicken breasts with olive oil, adobo, salt, and pepper. Place on top of the rice mixture.
Cover tightly with foil and bake 90 minutes until rice is fully cooked and chicken is done.
Remove foil, sprinkle cheddar on top of the chicken (not the rice), and bake uncovered 5 more minutes until melted.
recipes.serve_with: Habichuelas guisadas on the side for the full Puerto Rican plate
recipes.kiki_filter: APPROVED — cheese on top of the chicken only, not mixed into the rice. No spice, no pork, no cream. Minimal ingredients.

recipes.name: Chicken Teriyaki Ramen Stir-Fry
recipes.spanish_name: Ramen Salteado de Pollo Teriyaki
recipes.ibs_notes: Sticky sweet teriyaki glaze on chewy ramen noodles with tender chicken. Make the sauce in the same pan as the chicken — the browned bits add huge flavor. Do NOT use the ramen flavor packets. Use homemade teriyaki only.
ingredients: 2 packs instant ramen noodles (seasoning packets DISCARDED)
Sesame seeds and green onions to garnish
steps: Cook ramen noodles per package directions (discard flavor packets). Drain slightly underdone. Toss with a drop of sesame oil so they don't stick. Set aside.
Season chicken with salt and pepper. Cook in a hot oiled skillet over medium-high heat until golden and cooked through. Remove to a plate.
Mix teriyaki sauce, soy sauce, brown sugar, and garlic in the same pan. Simmer 1-2 minutes, scraping up the browned bits.
Add cornstarch slurry. Stir until sauce thickens and turns glossy.
Return chicken and noodles to the pan. Toss until everything is coated. Serve immediately topped with sesame seeds and green onions.
recipes.serve_with: Eat as a noodle bowl — it's a full meal on its own
recipes.kiki_filter: APPROVED — no spice, no sriracha, no red pepper. Skip those add-ons from any recipe version.

recipes.name: 15-Minute Teriyaki Chicken Bites over Rice
recipes.spanish_name: Trozos de Pollo Teriyaki en 15 Minutos con Arroz
recipes.ibs_notes: Fastest teriyaki possible. Cut chicken small so it cooks in under 10 minutes. Homemade sauce — no packets. Serve over instant white rice for a true 15-minute meal.
ingredients: 1.5 lbs chicken breast or thighs, cut into 1-inch cubes
Sesame seeds and green onions to garnish
steps: Mix soy sauce, honey, brown sugar, and garlic in a bowl. Set aside.
Heat oil in a skillet over high heat. Add chicken cubes in a single layer. Cook 3-4 minutes per side until golden and cooked through.
Pour sauce over chicken. Let bubble 1 minute. Add cornstarch slurry, stir until glossy and thick.
Serve immediately over white rice. Top with sesame seeds and green onions.
recipes.serve_with: Arroz blanco — the sauce soaks right in
recipes.ibs_adaptation: No spice. Low-sodium soy sauce keeps salt down. Honey and sugar are gentle sweeteners. Skip any version that adds sriracha.

recipes.name: Honey Teriyaki Salmon with White Rice
recipes.spanish_name: Salmón con Miel Teriyaki y Arroz Blanco
recipes.source_url: allrecipes.com fall chicken/salmon recipes + stir-fry collection
recipes.ibs_notes: Salmon is one of Kiki's approved proteins. Teriyaki glaze is gentle — no spice. Bake or pan-sear. Salmon + teriyaki + white rice is one of the best IBS-friendly combos.
ingredients: Sesame seeds and green onions to garnish
steps: Mix soy sauce, honey, brown sugar, garlic, and sesame oil in a small saucepan. Bring to a simmer. Add cornstarch slurry and stir until thickened. Set aside.
Pan-sear: Heat a skillet over medium-high heat with a little oil. Cook salmon skin-side up for 3-4 minutes. Flip and cook 2-3 more minutes.
Brush teriyaki glaze generously over each fillet. Cook 1 more minute until caramelized.
Serve over white rice. Garnish with sesame seeds and green onions.
recipes.serve_with: White rice — the glaze drips right into it perfectly
recipes.ibs_adaptation: Salmon is mild and IBS-friendly. No spice at all. Use low-sodium soy sauce.

recipes.name: Teriyaki Shrimp Stir-Fry over Rice
recipes.spanish_name: Camarones Teriyaki Salteados con Arroz
recipes.ibs_notes: Shrimp cooks in 3 minutes. Teriyaki sauce is mild and sweet. One of the quickest IBS-friendly dinners possible. Serve over white rice.
ingredients: 1 lb large shrimp, peeled and deveined
steps: Mix soy sauce, honey, brown sugar, and garlic. Set aside.
Heat oil in a skillet over high heat. Add shrimp in a single layer. Cook 1-2 minutes per side until pink. Remove to a plate.
Pour sauce into the same pan. Simmer 1 minute. Add cornstarch slurry, stir until glossy.
Return shrimp and toss to coat. Serve over white rice with sesame seeds.
recipes.serve_with: Arroz blanco
recipes.ibs_adaptation: No spice, no garlic overload — just a mild, sweet teriyaki glaze. Perfect for sensitive stomachs.

recipes.name: Teriyaki Ground Beef and Rice Bowl
recipes.spanish_name: Tazón de Carne Molida Teriyaki con Arroz
recipes.ibs_notes: Carne molida with a teriyaki twist — one of Kiki's favorite proteins in her favorite sauce. Ready in 20 minutes. The sauce glazes right into the meat over white rice.
ingredients: Green onions and sesame seeds to garnish
steps: Brown ground beef in a skillet over medium-high heat, breaking it up. Drain excess fat.
Mix soy sauce, honey, brown sugar, garlic, ginger, and sesame oil in a small bowl.
Pour sauce over beef. Stir and bring to a simmer for 2 minutes.
Add cornstarch slurry and stir until sauce thickens and coats the beef.
Serve over white rice. Garnish with green onions and sesame seeds.
recipes.serve_with: Arroz blanco — this is basically Asian picadillo
recipes.ibs_adaptation: No spice. Ginger is optional but actually gentle on IBS. Low-sodium soy sauce.

recipes.name: Teriyaki Chicken and Broccoli Skillet
recipes.spanish_name: Pollo Teriyaki con Brócoli en Sartén
recipes.source_url: allrecipes.com stir-fries for every night of the week collection
recipes.ibs_notes: Classic takeout combo made gentle. Broccoli is IBS-friendly when not overloaded. Skip any spice. Chicken thighs stay juicy. Everything in one pan.
ingredients: 1.5 lbs boneless skinless chicken thighs, cut into strips
White rice or noodles to serve
steps: Mix soy sauce, honey, brown sugar, and garlic. Set aside.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Season chicken with salt and pepper. Cook 4-5 minutes until golden. Remove to a plate.
In the same pan, add broccoli and 2 tbsp water. Cover and steam 3 minutes until bright green and tender-crisp.
Return chicken to pan. Pour sauce over everything. Bring to a simmer.
Add cornstarch slurry. Stir until sauce thickens and coats everything. Serve over rice or noodles.
recipes.serve_with: White rice or spaghetti noodles
recipes.ibs_adaptation: No spice. Broccoli steamed not fried — gentler on the stomach. Keep portions moderate.

recipes.name: Teriyaki Steak Bites with Fried Rice
recipes.spanish_name: Bocados de Bistec Teriyaki con Arroz Frito
recipes.ibs_notes: Steak in a teriyaki glaze over simple fried rice — Kiki's two favorites in one dish. Sear steak hot and fast to keep it tender. Use leftover rice for the best fried rice texture.
ingredients: 1.5 lbs sirloin steak, cut into bite-size pieces
2 cups cooked white rice (day-old is best)
steps: Mix soy sauce, honey, brown sugar, and garlic for teriyaki sauce. Set aside.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a wok or large skillet over very high heat. Sear steak pieces 1-2 minutes per side until browned but not overcooked. Pour teriyaki sauce over, toss to coat, and remove to a plate.
In the same pan, add remaining oil. Add rice and stir-fry 2 minutes, breaking up any clumps.
Push rice to the side. Scramble eggs in the empty space until just set. Mix into rice.
Drizzle sesame oil over everything. Serve steak bites over the fried rice. Top with green onions.
recipes.serve_with: Eat as a complete bowl
recipes.ibs_adaptation: No spice. High heat and quick cook keeps the steak tender. Sesame oil is the only addition — very gentle.

recipes.name: One-Pan Teriyaki Chicken Thighs with Roasted Potatoes
recipes.spanish_name: Muslos de Pollo Teriyaki con Papas Asadas en Una Sola Sartén
recipes.source_url: allrecipes.com fall chicken + 5-ingredient one-pan dinner collections
recipes.ibs_notes: Chicken thighs and potatoes — both of Kiki's favorites — baked together with teriyaki glaze. The sauce caramelizes in the oven into something incredible. One pan, easy cleanup.
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F.
Mix soy sauce, honey, brown sugar, and garlic. Set aside half for basting.
Toss potato wedges with olive oil, salt, and pepper on a large baking sheet. Roast 15 minutes.
Season chicken with salt and pepper. Nestle into the pan with the potatoes. Brush generously with teriyaki sauce.
Bake 30-35 minutes, basting chicken with remaining sauce halfway through, until chicken reaches 165°F and potatoes are golden.
Garnish with sesame seeds and serve from the pan.
recipes.serve_with: Complete meal as-is. Add habichuelas on the side if you want
recipes.ibs_adaptation: No spice. Oven-baked is the most IBS-gentle cooking method. The glaze caramelizes without burning.

recipes.name: Teriyaki Chicken Fried Rice
recipes.spanish_name: Arroz Frito de Pollo Teriyaki
recipes.ibs_notes: Everything in one pan. Use day-old rice — it won't get mushy. Teriyaki sauce glazes right into the rice. Eggs add protein. No spice needed.
ingredients: 1 lb chicken breast or thighs, diced small
Green onions and sesame seeds to garnish
steps: Mix soy sauce, honey, brown sugar, and garlic for the teriyaki sauce.
Heat 1 tbsp oil over high heat. Cook diced chicken until golden and cooked through, about 5 minutes. Remove to a plate.
Add remaining oil to the pan. Add rice and stir-fry 2 minutes breaking up lumps.
Push rice aside. Pour in eggs and scramble until just set. Mix into rice.
Return chicken. Pour teriyaki sauce over everything and toss 1-2 minutes until everything is glazed.
Drizzle sesame oil. Garnish with green onions and sesame seeds.
recipes.serve_with: Complete meal on its own
recipes.ibs_adaptation: Day-old rice makes this much better and easier to digest than freshly cooked. No spice.

recipes.name: Teriyaki Beef Noodle Stir-Fry
recipes.spanish_name: Fideos con Carne Teriyaki Salteados
recipes.source_url: allrecipes.com stir-fries for every night of the week collection
recipes.ibs_notes: Ground beef or thinly sliced sirloin both work great. The teriyaki sauce coats every noodle. Use spaghetti, udon, or ramen — whatever Kiki has at home. No spice version.
ingredients: 1 lb ground beef or thinly sliced sirloin
8 oz spaghetti, udon, or ramen noodles
Green onions and sesame seeds to garnish
steps: Cook noodles per package, drain, toss with a drop of sesame oil so they don't stick.
Mix soy sauce, honey, brown sugar, garlic, and sesame oil.
Brown beef in a hot skillet. Drain fat if needed.
Pour sauce over beef. Simmer 2 minutes. Add cornstarch slurry until thickened.
Toss in noodles and mix until completely coated. Garnish and serve.
recipes.serve_with: Eat as a noodle bowl
recipes.ibs_adaptation: No spice, no fish sauce, no sriracha. Low-sodium soy sauce. Perfectly mild.

recipes.name: Slow-Cooker Teriyaki Chicken
recipes.spanish_name: Pollo Teriyaki en Slow Cooker
recipes.ibs_notes: Hands-off cooking at its best. Chicken becomes so tender it practically shreds itself. The slow cooker makes this one of the most IBS-friendly cooking methods — extremely tender, easy to digest. Great for meal prep.
ingredients: 2 lbs boneless skinless chicken breasts or thighs
2 tbsp cornstarch + 3 tbsp water (add at the end)
Sesame seeds and green onions to garnish
steps: Place chicken in the slow cooker.
Mix soy sauce, honey, brown sugar, garlic, ginger, and sesame oil. Pour over chicken.
Cook on LOW 5-6 hours or HIGH 3-4 hours until chicken is very tender.
Remove chicken and shred or slice.
Pour cooking liquid into a saucepan. Bring to a simmer. Add cornstarch slurry and stir until thickened into a glaze.
Return chicken to sauce. Serve over white rice with sesame seeds and green onions.
recipes.serve_with: Arroz blanco — shredded teriyaki chicken over white rice is incredible
recipes.ibs_adaptation: Slow-cooked meat is the most tender and digestible. No spice. Shredded texture is very gentle on the stomach.

recipes.name: Teriyaki Chicken Meatballs over Rice
recipes.spanish_name: Albóndigas de Pollo Teriyaki con Arroz
recipes.ibs_notes: Ground chicken meatballs glazed in teriyaki sauce. Fun, different, and very IBS-gentle. Bake the meatballs instead of frying for even gentler results.
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F. Mix ground chicken, breadcrumbs, egg, garlic, salt, and pepper. Roll into 1-inch balls. Place on a greased baking sheet.
Bake 15-18 minutes until cooked through and lightly golden.
Meanwhile, simmer soy sauce, honey, brown sugar, and sesame oil in a saucepan. Add cornstarch slurry until glossy.
Toss baked meatballs in the teriyaki glaze. Serve over white rice.
recipes.serve_with: Arroz blanco
recipes.ibs_adaptation: Baked not fried. Ground chicken is very lean and IBS-friendly. No spice.

recipes.name: Honey Garlic Chicken (Soy Sauce Based)
recipes.spanish_name: Pollo con Miel y Ajo (con Salsa de Soya)
recipes.ibs_notes: One of the best 5-ingredient quick dinners. Honey + soy + garlic is a classic IBS-safe sauce. No spice. No cream. Serve over rice for a complete meal in 15 minutes.
ingredients: 1.5 lbs chicken breast or thighs, cut into pieces
steps: Mix soy sauce, honey, and garlic in a bowl.
Season chicken with salt and pepper. Heat oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Cook chicken 4-5 minutes per side until golden and cooked through.
Pour honey garlic sauce over chicken. Cook 1-2 minutes, turning to coat, until sauce caramelizes.
Serve over white rice.
recipes.serve_with: Arroz blanco — the sauce is perfect for the rice
recipes.ibs_adaptation: Only 3 sauce ingredients. No spice, no butter, no cream. Extremely gentle and quick.

recipes.name: Soy Sauce Butter Noodles with Chicken
recipes.spanish_name: Fideos con Mantequilla y Salsa de Soya con Pollo
recipes.ibs_notes: Butter + soy sauce + garlic on noodles is one of the most comforting, stomach-gentle combinations possible. It's basically garlic noodles. Add chicken for protein.
ingredients: Parmesan cheese or sesame seeds to garnish (optional)
steps: Cook pasta per package directions. Reserve 1/4 cup pasta water. Drain.
Season chicken with salt and pepper. Cook in a skillet with 1 tbsp butter over medium-high heat until golden. Remove.
In the same pan, melt remaining butter. Add garlic and sauté 1 minute until fragrant.
Add soy sauce and pasta water. Stir to combine.
Toss noodles in the sauce until coated. Top with chicken. Garnish with sesame seeds or parmesan.
recipes.serve_with: Eat as a noodle bowl. Add steamed broccoli on the side if you want vegetables.
recipes.ibs_adaptation: Butter is generally well-tolerated. Keep butter portions reasonable. No spice.

recipes.name: Soy Garlic Salmon over White Rice
recipes.spanish_name: Salmón al Ajo y Soya con Arroz Blanco
recipes.ibs_notes: Salmon is Kiki's go-to fish. Soy + garlic + a touch of honey is gentle, flavorful, and ready fast. Pan-sear for crispy edges.
ingredients: Lemon wedges and green onions to serve
steps: Mix soy sauce, honey, garlic, and sesame oil.
Heat oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Cook salmon skin-side down 3-4 minutes. Flip.
Pour sauce over salmon. Cook 2-3 more minutes, spooning sauce over the top, until cooked through and glazed.
Serve over white rice with lemon wedges.
recipes.serve_with: Arroz blanco
recipes.ibs_adaptation: Salmon is one of the most IBS-friendly proteins. This sauce is simple and mild — no spice at all.

recipes.name: Korean-Style Beef Rice Bowl (Bulgogi-Inspired)
recipes.spanish_name: Tazón de Carne Estilo Coreano (Inspirado en Bulgogi)
recipes.ibs_notes: Soy sauce + brown sugar + garlic + sesame oil — basically a mild bulgogi. Ground beef version is even faster than sliced beef. Very IBS-safe. No spice.
ingredients: 1 lb ground beef or thinly sliced sirloin
Green onions and sesame seeds to garnish
steps: Mix soy sauce, brown sugar, sesame oil, garlic, and ginger.
Brown beef in a skillet over medium-high heat. Drain fat.
Pour sauce over beef. Stir and simmer 2-3 minutes until beef absorbs the glaze.
Serve over white rice. Top with green onions and sesame seeds.
recipes.serve_with: White rice — best eaten as a rice bowl
recipes.ibs_adaptation: No gochujang, no spice of any kind. This is the mild version of bulgogi that's fully IBS-friendly.

recipes.name: Soy Ginger Chicken Thighs Baked
recipes.spanish_name: Muslos de Pollo al Horno con Soya y Jengibre
recipes.ibs_notes: Ginger is actually soothing for IBS — it helps with digestion. Soy + ginger + garlic baked low and slow makes the most tender chicken. Great for meal prep.
steps: Mix soy sauce, honey, olive oil, garlic, and ginger. Marinate chicken for 30 minutes if time allows, or just coat and bake.
Preheat oven to 400°F. Place chicken skin-side up in a baking dish.
Bake 35-40 minutes until skin is golden and internal temp is 165°F. Baste with pan juices halfway through.
recipes.serve_with: Arroz blanco, mashed potatoes, or roasted potatoes
recipes.ibs_adaptation: Ginger aids digestion. Baked is IBS-gentlest. Simple sauce, no spice.

recipes.name: Soy Sauce Shrimp and Noodle Bowl
recipes.spanish_name: Tazón de Camarones y Fideos con Soya
recipes.ibs_notes: Shrimp + soy sauce + noodles in under 20 minutes. Garlic and butter make it rich without being heavy. No cream, no spice. Perfect light dinner.
ingredients: Green onions and sesame seeds to garnish
steps: Cook noodles, drain, and set aside.
Melt butter in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add garlic, sauté 1 minute.
Add shrimp, cook 1-2 minutes per side until pink.
Add soy sauce and sesame oil. Toss noodles in the pan and mix everything together 1 minute.
Serve topped with green onions and sesame seeds.
recipes.serve_with: Eat as a noodle bowl
recipes.ibs_adaptation: Very light sauce. No cream or heavy ingredients. Shrimp cooks fast — don't overcook.

recipes.name: Honey Soy Glazed Chicken Wings
recipes.spanish_name: Alitas de Pollo Glaseadas con Miel y Soya
recipes.ibs_notes: Baked wings with a sweet honey-soy glaze — no buffalo sauce, no hot sauce. Kiki can enjoy wings without the spice. Baking makes them IBS-gentler than frying.
ingredients: Sesame seeds and green onions to garnish
steps: Preheat oven to 425°F. Toss wings with olive oil, salt, and pepper. Bake on a rack 35 minutes until crispy.
Mix soy sauce, honey, brown sugar, and garlic in a saucepan. Simmer 2-3 minutes until slightly thickened.
Toss baked wings in the glaze. Return to oven 5 more minutes until caramelized.
Garnish with sesame seeds and green onions.
recipes.serve_with: White rice or eat alone as a meal
recipes.ibs_adaptation: No buffalo, no hot sauce, no spice. Pure sweet-savory glaze. Baked not fried.

recipes.name: Chicken and Shrimp Garlic Ginger Stir-Fry
recipes.spanish_name: Salteado de Pollo y Camarones con Ajo y Jengibre
recipes.source_url: allrecipes.com stir-fries for every night of the week
recipes.ibs_notes: Both of Kiki's approved proteins in one stir-fry. Garlic-ginger sauce is one of the most IBS-friendly flavors. No spice, no hot oil. Serve over white rice.
ingredients: 1 lb boneless chicken thighs, sliced thin
steps: Mix soy sauce, honey, garlic, ginger, and sesame oil for the sauce.
Heat oil over high heat. Cook chicken 3-4 minutes until golden. Add shrimp, cook 2 more minutes until pink. Remove both.
Pour sauce into pan. Simmer 1 minute. Add cornstarch slurry until glossy.
Return chicken and shrimp. Toss to coat. Serve over white rice.
recipes.serve_with: Arroz blanco
recipes.ibs_adaptation: Ginger soothes the stomach. No spice. Light sauce, not heavy or creamy.

recipes.name: Beef and Broccoli Stir-Fry (Soy Sauce Based)
recipes.spanish_name: Salteado de Carne y Brócoli con Salsa de Soya
recipes.ibs_notes: Classic takeout-style dish made at home without the heavy sauce. Thin strips of sirloin stay tender. Broccoli adds nutrients without triggering IBS when kept to moderate portions.
ingredients: 1 lb sirloin steak, sliced thin against the grain
1 tbsp oyster sauce (optional — omit if sensitive)
steps: Mix soy sauce, oyster sauce, brown sugar, garlic, and sesame oil.
Steam broccoli 3 minutes until bright green and tender-crisp. Set aside.
Heat oil over very high heat. Sear steak strips 1-2 minutes per side. Remove.
Add sauce to pan. Simmer. Add cornstarch slurry until thickened.
Return steak and broccoli. Toss to coat. Serve over white rice.
recipes.serve_with: Arroz blanco
recipes.ibs_adaptation: No spice. Skip oyster sauce if you prefer — soy sauce alone works great. Steam broccoli instead of stir-frying raw for easier digestion.

recipes.name: Garlic Butter Shrimp Stir-Fry over White Rice
recipes.spanish_name: Camarones al Ajillo Salteados con Arroz Blanco
recipes.ibs_notes: Garlic butter shrimp is one of the most IBS-friendly meals possible — fast, light, and delicious. A drop of soy sauce adds depth without making it heavy.
ingredients: 1 lb large shrimp, peeled and deveined
steps: Melt butter in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add garlic and sauté 1 minute.
Add shrimp in a single layer. Season with salt and pepper. Cook 1-2 minutes per side until pink.
Splash in soy sauce. Squeeze lemon juice over everything. Toss to coat.
Serve immediately over white rice. Garnish with parsley.
recipes.serve_with: Arroz blanco — the garlic butter pools right into the rice
recipes.ibs_adaptation: Butter is fine for most IBS. Garlic is the star — rich flavor without spice. Super quick and gentle.

recipes.name: Simple Chicken Stir-Fry with Soy Sauce
recipes.spanish_name: Salteado Simple de Pollo con Salsa de Soya
recipes.source_url: allrecipes.com stir-fries for every night of the week
recipes.ibs_notes: The simplest possible stir-fry — chicken, soy, garlic, sesame oil. Nothing complicated. Perfect for when Kiki is tired and just needs something good fast.
steps: Mix soy sauce, honey, garlic, and sesame oil.
Heat oil in a skillet over high heat. Cook chicken strips 3-4 minutes until cooked through.
Pour sauce over chicken. Toss 1 minute until glazed.
Serve over white rice.
recipes.serve_with: Arroz blanco
recipes.ibs_adaptation: As simple as it gets. Four sauce ingredients. No spice. Ready in 15 minutes.

recipes.name: Sheet Pan Beef and Broccoli
recipes.spanish_name: Carne y Brócoli en Bandeja al Horno
recipes.ibs_notes: Oven version of beef and broccoli — no wok needed, less oil, gentler on the stomach. Soy sauce glaze caramelizes in the oven beautifully.
steps: Preheat oven to 425°F.
Mix soy sauce, honey, brown sugar, garlic, olive oil, and sesame oil.
Toss steak and broccoli together in the sauce. Spread on a lined baking sheet.
Bake 12-15 minutes, tossing once, until beef is cooked and broccoli is caramelized at the edges.
Serve over white rice.
recipes.serve_with: Arroz blanco
recipes.ibs_adaptation: Baking instead of stir-frying uses less oil and is gentler. No spice.

recipes.name: Mongolian Beef Noodles (15 Minutes)
recipes.spanish_name: Fideos de Carne Estilo Mongol (15 Minutos)
recipes.ibs_notes: Ground beef, soy sauce, brown sugar, and noodles — done in 15 minutes. No spice needed. Hoisin sauce adds depth — use a small amount and skip if sensitive.
ingredients: Green onions and sesame seeds to garnish
steps: Boil pasta while making the beef — both take about 10 minutes.
Brown ground beef. Drain fat. Add garlic and cook 1 minute.
Add soy sauce, hoisin, brown sugar, and sesame oil. Stir and simmer 2 minutes.
Toss in drained noodles and mix everything together.
Garnish and serve.
recipes.serve_with: Complete bowl on its own
recipes.ibs_adaptation: Hoisin is optional — skip it if it bothers you. The dish is great with just soy sauce and brown sugar.

recipes.name: Soy Garlic Ramen Noodle Bowl with Chicken
recipes.spanish_name: Tazón de Ramen con Pollo al Ajo y Soya
recipes.source_url: allrecipes.com easy ramen with chicken 284318 + 10-minute collections
recipes.ibs_notes: Upgrade ramen from a packet to a real meal. Discard the flavor packets completely — they're loaded with sodium and MSG. Make your own mild soy-garlic broth instead.
ingredients: 2 packs instant ramen noodles (flavor packets DISCARDED)
1 cup shredded cooked chicken (rotisserie is perfect)
Green onions and sesame seeds to garnish
steps: Bring chicken broth, soy sauce, garlic, and sesame oil to a simmer.
Add ramen noodles and cook 2-3 minutes until tender.
Divide into bowls. Top with shredded chicken, a halved soft-boiled egg if using, green onions, and sesame seeds.
recipes.serve_with: Eat as a full soup bowl
recipes.ibs_adaptation: Homemade broth is so much gentler than the sodium-bomb packets. Light and soothing. Perfect for bad stomach days.

recipes.name: Soy Butter Shrimp Pasta
recipes.spanish_name: Pasta de Camarones con Mantequilla y Soya
recipes.ibs_notes: Shrimp, spaghetti, butter, soy sauce, and garlic. Five ingredients. This is Kiki's comfort food meeting Asian flavors — totally IBS-safe.
ingredients: Parmesan or sesame seeds to garnish
steps: Cook spaghetti. Reserve 1/4 cup pasta water. Drain.
Melt butter in a skillet. Add garlic, sauté 1 minute.
Add shrimp, cook 1-2 min per side until pink. Season with salt and pepper.
Add soy sauce and pasta water. Toss noodles in the pan until silky and coated.
Serve with a sprinkle of parmesan or sesame seeds.
recipes.serve_with: Complete meal on its own
recipes.ibs_adaptation: Butter-soy is extremely gentle. No cream, no spice. Simple and satisfying.

recipes.name: 9x13 Soy-Glazed Chicken Thigh Bake
recipes.spanish_name: Muslos de Pollo Glaseados con Soya en Bandeja 9x13
recipes.ibs_notes: Throw chicken thighs in a 9x13, pour sauce over top, bake. The sauce caramelizes into a glaze. As easy as it gets and extremely IBS-friendly.
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F.
Mix soy sauce, honey, brown sugar, garlic, olive oil, and sesame oil.
Season chicken with salt and pepper. Place skin-side up in a 9x13 pan.
Pour sauce over chicken. Bake 40-45 minutes, basting twice, until golden and cooked through.
Garnish with sesame seeds. Serve with the pan juices drizzled over.
recipes.serve_with: Arroz blanco and habichuelas — full plate
recipes.ibs_adaptation: Baked = gentlest cooking method. No spice. The sweet-savory glaze is mild and delicious.

recipes.name: Ground Beef Taco Casserole (9x13)
recipes.spanish_name: Casserole de Tacos de Carne Molida (9x13)
recipes.ibs_notes: All the flavors of tacos in a baked casserole. Use mild taco seasoning — just cumin, garlic powder, onion powder, no chili. Cheddar or monterey jack cheese on top. No spice.
ingredients: 1.5 cups shredded cheddar or monterey jack cheese
steps: Preheat oven to 375°F. Grease a 9x13 pan.
Brown ground beef. Drain fat. Add cumin, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and pepper.
Stir in tomatoes, black beans, and corn. Simmer 5 minutes.
Layer: torn tortillas on the bottom, half the beef mixture, repeat, finish with beef mixture on top.
Cover with cheese. Bake 20-25 minutes until bubbly and golden.
recipes.serve_with: Eat straight from the pan. Add sour cream on the side if tolerated.
recipes.ibs_adaptation: NO chili powder, NO hot sauce, NO jalapeños. Just cumin, garlic, onion powder — IBS-friendly taco flavors.

recipes.name: Honey Garlic Shrimp and Rice Skillet
recipes.spanish_name: Sartén de Camarones con Miel, Ajo y Arroz
recipes.ibs_notes: One skillet — shrimp and rice cook together. The honey-garlic sauce goes right into the rice as it cooks. Five ingredients, one pan, amazing result.
ingredients: Lemon juice and parsley to serve
steps: Melt butter in a large skillet. Add garlic and sauté 1 minute.
Add rice, stir to coat. Pour in chicken broth, honey, and soy sauce. Bring to a boil.
Cover, reduce to low, cook 15-18 minutes until rice is tender.
Nestle shrimp into the rice, cover, cook 3-4 more minutes until shrimp are pink.
Squeeze lemon over the top. Garnish with parsley.
recipes.serve_with: Complete one-pan meal
recipes.ibs_adaptation: Everything cooks together in one pan. No spice, no heavy cream. Broth and honey keep it gentle.

recipes.name: Chicken Teriyaki Casserole (9x13)
recipes.spanish_name: Casserole de Pollo Teriyaki (9x13)
recipes.ibs_notes: All the teriyaki flavor baked hands-off in a casserole dish. Chicken thighs + white rice + teriyaki sauce all cook together. The rice absorbs the sauce as it bakes.
ingredients: Sesame seeds and green onions to garnish
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease a 9x13 pan.
Mix chicken broth, soy sauce, honey, brown sugar, garlic, and sesame oil. Pour into the pan with uncooked rice — stir to distribute.
Place chicken thighs on top of the rice mixture.
Cover tightly with foil. Bake 45-50 minutes until rice is cooked and chicken is done.
Remove foil, baste chicken with the pan juices, return to oven uncovered 5 minutes.
Garnish with sesame seeds and green onions.
recipes.serve_with: Complete meal in one pan
recipes.ibs_adaptation: Hands-off baking — no standing over a hot stove. Rice and chicken cook in the mild teriyaki broth. No spice.

recipes.name: Honey Lemon Chicken with Roasted Potatoes
recipes.spanish_name: Pollo con Miel y Limón y Papas Asadas
recipes.ibs_notes: Lemon + honey + a little soy sauce = one of the best sauces for IBS. Lemon aids digestion. Everything on one pan. Two of Kiki's absolute favorites — chicken and potatoes.
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F.
Mix lemon juice, honey, soy sauce, garlic, and olive oil.
Toss potatoes with half the sauce, salt, and pepper. Spread on a baking sheet. Roast 15 minutes.
Add chicken to the pan. Pour remaining sauce over everything.
Bake 25-30 minutes until chicken is done and potatoes are golden.
Garnish with parsley.
recipes.serve_with: Complete meal as-is. Add habichuelas on the side.
recipes.ibs_adaptation: Lemon is IBS-soothing. Honey adds sweetness without sugar spikes. No spice at all.

recipes.name: Soy Sauce Chicken with Mashed Potatoes
recipes.spanish_name: Pollo con Salsa de Soya y Puré de Papas
recipes.ibs_notes: Pan sauce made with soy sauce, butter, and garlic poured over the chicken and spooned over mashed potatoes. The soy-butter sauce on mashed potatoes is incredibly good.
ingredients: 4 large potatoes, peeled and cubed (for mashed potatoes)
3 tbsp butter + 1/2 cup warm milk (for mash)
steps: Boil potatoes until fork-tender. Drain. Mash with butter, warm milk, salt, and garlic powder.
Season chicken with salt and pepper. Cook in a skillet with 1 tbsp butter over medium-high heat, 3-4 minutes per side. Remove.
In the same pan, add garlic and sauté 1 minute. Add soy sauce and honey. Stir.
Return chicken to the pan. Spoon sauce over the top.
Serve chicken over mashed potatoes with sauce drizzled over everything.
recipes.serve_with: The mashed potatoes ARE the side — this is the full plate
recipes.ibs_adaptation: Mashed potatoes are one of the best IBS foods. The soy-butter sauce is gentle and flavorful.

recipes.name: Quick Beef Stir-Fry with Soy and Potatoes
recipes.spanish_name: Salteado Rápido de Carne con Soya y Papas
recipes.ibs_notes: A Puerto Rican twist on a stir-fry — beef, potatoes, and soy sauce instead of just rice. Boil the potatoes first so they're soft. Kiki's comfort food in a new form.
ingredients: 1 lb ground beef or thinly sliced sirloin
steps: Boil potatoes until fork-tender. Drain and cube.
Brown beef in olive oil over medium-high heat. Drain excess fat.
Add garlic and cook 1 minute. Add soy sauce and brown sugar. Stir.
Add boiled potato cubes. Toss everything together and cook 2-3 minutes until potatoes absorb some of the sauce.
Garnish with green onions. Serve as-is or over white rice.
recipes.serve_with: Arroz blanco on the side, or eat alone
recipes.ibs_adaptation: Boiling potatoes first makes them very easy to digest. No spice. A grounding, familiar combo.
